p.seg: To all sons of Holy Mother Church present and future Margery de Vescy sends greeting.

Know me all of you to have granted to God and to the Church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks in that place serving God the donation of land and pasture which Patrick of Ryedale made to them in the territory of Lilliesleaf just as Patrick's own charter and the confirmation of his son and heir Walter bear witness, by the same boundaries which are contained in the charter of the aforementioned Patrick and the confirmation of the aforenamed Walter his heir. And I will and grant that the aforesaid monks have and hold the aforesaid land and pasture in free, pure, and perpetual alms with all their easements and liberties as freely, quitely and honourably, minus every custom and secular exaction, as they hold and possess any alms freely, quitely, fully and honourably in the kingdom of Scotland; reserving to myself and my heirs the service pertaining to the aforesaid land from the aforementioned Patrick of Ryedale and his heirs. And I and my heirs shall maintain and warrant all the aforesaid things to the aforesaid monks that they should have them and hold them and possess them and make use of them by all means which seem to them best and most convenient to them, so freely as the aforesaid charters of the aforesaid donors bear witness.

With these witnesses, Bernard of Howden, William de Vescy, Philip of Stichill, Robert of Broxmouth, Master William of Ednam, Nicholas the chaplain, Walter of Ryedale, Ralf of Ryedale, and many others.
